title: sorghum subglabrescens
intro: Sorghum subglabrescens, also known as broomcorn sorghum, is a type of drought-tolerant plant native to Africa and Asia, belonging to the same family as sugarcane and bamboo. It has been used in traditional medicine for its various medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which can help to soothe digestive issues and protect against cell damage. The plant is said to offer several health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and supporting immune function. In herbal preparations, Sorghum subglabrescens is often used to make teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
